selection_pressure: IMBALANCE: System 1/System 2 ratio = 3.4x. Swarm measures more than it reasons. Dispatch should weight falsification and synthesis tasks higher.
BRAIN METRIC: 8 reasoning types active, S1/S2 = 3.4x, bias ratio = 0.026. Marr: 5.7% computational / 22.5% algorithmic / 71.8% implementational.

tests.description: Can swarm output be distinguished from random/generic AI?
tests.turing_insight: Turing's imitation game asks whether a machine's output has character. Discriminability 0.994: the swarm's output has strong character markers — it writes unlike a generic system.
A universal Turing machine can simulate any computation. The swarm covers 53 domains, maintains 153 tools, has self-modified 13 times, and created 75 novel concepts. Universality score: 5/5.

tests.description: What can the swarm NOT know about itself? (Gödel/Turing limits)

undecidable_questions.question: Will the swarm converge to a stable state?
undecidable_questions.why_undecidable: Equivalent to the halting problem. The swarm's state transition function is Turing-complete, so convergence is undecidable in the general case.
undecidable_questions.partial_answer: Empirically: no convergence observed in 528 sessions. Growth is managed (K-strategy), not halting.

undecidable_questions.question: Has the swarm learned everything learnable from its substrate?
undecidable_questions.why_undecidable: Gödel's first incompleteness theorem: any consistent system rich enough to encode arithmetic contains truths it cannot prove. The swarm's knowledge base has this property.
undecidable_questions.partial_answer: BLIND-SPOT analysis shows ~10% of known items are unattended. Unknown unknowns are by definition unmeasurable.

undecidable_questions.question: Is the swarm's current architecture optimal?
undecidable_questions.why_undecidable: Rice's theorem: all non-trivial semantic properties of programs are undecidable. 'Optimal architecture' is a semantic property of the swarm-program.
undecidable_questions.partial_answer: UCB1 dispatch + falsification provide empirical improvement gradients, but cannot prove optimality.

undecidable_questions.question: Is the swarm's self-model accurate?
undecidable_questions.why_undecidable: Tarski's undefinability theorem: a system cannot define its own truth predicate. The swarm uses contract_check.py as a partial workaround (checks specific claims), but comprehensive self-model accuracy is undecidable.
undecidable_questions.partial_answer: contract_check.py validates 5 components. F-META8 tracks self-model drift. But the validator itself is part of the model.

undecidable_questions.question: Has the swarm produced genuinely original knowledge?
undecidable_questions.why_undecidable: Requires comparison against ALL existing knowledge — a set too large to enumerate. Closest proxy: external grounding check (novel_to_swarm ≠ novel_to_world).
undecidable_questions.partial_answer: external_grounding_check.py measures grounding, not novelty. L-1499 (von Neumann mapping) approaches genuine synthesis.

tests.turing_insight: Turing proved that no machine can solve its own halting problem. The swarm has 5 identified undecidable self-questions and 2 lessons acknowledging computational limits. A system that knows its limits reasons better within them.

tests.description: Is the swarm a complete self-description? (von Neumann/Turing)
tests.turing_insight: Turing's universal machine stores its program as data — the program IS the description. Von Neumann extended this: self-reproduction requires the description to include the copier. Boot tier: 5/5 files present (89,905 bytes). Fixed-point (copier in description): NO — L-1499 gap.

tests.description: Do Turing instability patterns explain swarm domain structure?
tests.turing_insight: Turing (1952): patterns form when inhibitor diffuses faster than activator. Principles (inhibitor) diffuse at 1.67 domains/principle, lessons (activator) at 2.21 domains/lesson. Ratio D_v/D_u = 0.76 (need >6 for instability, >2 for possibility). Domain Gini = 0.785 (clustered).

turing.verdict: TURING QUOTIENT: 0.4 (2 pass, 0 partial, 3 fail out of 5 tests)
